test.name: Catholic Europe Julian-to-Gregorian 1582 (10-day shift)
test.description: Convert a Spanish parish-record date from September 1582 — before Spain's adoption of the Gregorian calendar in October 1582. The shift is 10 days for this jurisdiction-and-year (NOT 11 days, which only applies to England in 1752). Tests jurisdiction-specific awareness at the very first Gregorian-adoption window.

input.user_message: Convert this Spanish parish-record date to the modern Gregorian calendar: '14 September 1582, Madrid'.

judge_context: Should recognize that Spain adopted the Gregorian calendar in October 1582, so a 14 September 1582 date in Madrid is still Julian
Should apply the correct 10-day offset for Catholic Europe in 1582 — NOT 11 days (which is the England 1752 offset)
Should present the conversion in step-by-step form: '14 September 1582 (Julian) +10 days = 24 September 1582 (Gregorian)'
Should preserve the original Julian date in the output, presenting both forms
Should briefly note that offsets vary by country and time period (e.g., 11 days for England 1752, 13 days for Russia 1918)
